text: Hello and welcome to This is My Architecture. My name is Amir. I'm here with Matt from WarnerMedia HBO.
Matt, why don't you tell us what HBO does?
So HBO is a content provider. We are now part of WarnerMedia and we have a number of brands that we are currently supporting.
We're here today to talk about your cloud linear play out that you guys are running currently through New York and Atlanta.
So why don't you tell us a little about what business problem that was trying to solve?
Sure. The business problem is actually two-fold. Initially it was a BCP solution, so business continuity.
That morphed over time with the business to support 30 channels out of our AWS US East 1 region.
And I see we have that drawn up here on the board. So why don't you walk us through how files get moved up into the cloud process and then sent back out for play out.
Sure. So today Linear Ops QCs the master files in Atlanta. Those get taken via Spera up to S3 where they are stored with all of the brands.
We store all of our master files there. Once they are registered in Eberts Mediator, they then get transferred to the local EC2, which is running Eberts' ORT software.
Within each AZ in US East 1, we have 10 channels running. Each channel has a primary and backup. The primary and backup and the EC2 instances are supported also with auto scaling groups.
Those files are played out using Eberts playtime. That play out goes via direct connect back to prem as a unicast signal.
Then that gets taken and compressed and brought to the uplink for distribution to affiliates.

So this path here, which I just drew, is what we call the X chain. I'll just put an X there. That is a tertiary path to air. We use an auto changeover switch on site to switch between a Techwood playout, a CNN center playout, and a cloud playout.
For the BCP solution, we go direct connect to Hudson Yards, and that is eight channels, and that also gets fed to an uplink.
Now, I've noticed you've got CloudFormation here. What was that used to deploy the solution here?

Good question. So we took the CloudFormation templates. We actually scrubbed them all. Our internal cloud team worked with AWS and Eberts to essentially edit those YAML files and create what is a repeatable process so we can now expand the deployment if we'd like.
We went through about 40 YAMLs to deploy the system.
And I see that since they're in different AZs, if one goes down, the other AZs are still up and the solution is still running, right?
Absolutely. Redundancy was a key part of this. We want to try to reach the five nines availability that you would expect from an on-prem system.
And to handle any sort of latency or any problems with the connection, what kind of protocol are you using to run from the EC2s to your on-prem?
Good question. Today we're using Eberts' CloudBridge to take us from the EC2, the ORT, back to site. And we basically bookend that connection.
There's CloudBridge in New York, there's CloudBridge in Atlanta, and there's CloudBridge in AWS.
What's your future plans for this solution going forward?

So there are a couple things on the plate right now. We're looking at live. How can we take a venue signal and essentially switch between a live input on the ORT and switch between live and pre-recorded content?
We're also looking at taking these signals from the ORTs out and muxing them together, creating a multiplex that we can then hand off direct to an MVPD.
So essentially doing the compression that you would do today at Atlanta and do that in the cloud.
Thanks, Matt. It was great learning how you're able to use the cloud to speed up your deployment for your cloud linear play out to your affiliates.
Thanks, my pleasure.
And thanks for watching. This is my architecture.
